item.title: 5 suspects nabbed for illegal cable tapping in Parañaque; over ₱201K in copper cables recovered
item.description: The National Capital Region Police Office has arrested five individuals for alleged illegal cable tapping and theft of underground copper cables along West Service Road in Barangay Marcelo Green, Parañaque City on Sunday, March 22, said Metro Manila top cop PMGen Anthony Aberin.

item.title: Palace: Marcos ‘means no offense to Commission on Appointments’ in having several acting secretaries
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. “means no offense to the Commission on Appointments (CA)” in his decision to place officers in charge as heads of executive departments instead of full-fledged Cabinet Secretaries, Malacañang said on Monday.

item.title: PNP urges motorists to stay patient, prioritize safety during Holy Week rush
item.description: The Philippine National Police-Highway Patrol Group (PNP-HPG) on Monday urged motorists to remain patient and prioritize road safety as a surge in travelers is expected ahead of the Holy Week and summer vacation.

item.title: Up to Ombudsman to review alleged ICI findings clearing Romualdez in flood control mess: Palace
item.description: Tingog Party-list Rep. Jude Acidre had claimed that the ICI had cleared former House Speaker Ferdinand Martin Romualdez — President Ferdinand Marcos Jr.'s cousin — in their reports surrounding billions worth of anomalous flood control projects.

item.title: Palace defends Marcos attendance in aid distribution despite ‘anti-epal’ policy
item.description: It is necessary for President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. to appear in cash distribution sites to demonstrate to the public that the “government is running,” Malacañang said on Monday, when asked why several politicians — including the Chief Executive — were allowed to visit a cash aid distribution site.

item.title: Rhian Ramos to pursue possible legal action over driver’s assault claims
item.description: Actress Rhian Ramos said she is already looking into possible legal actions against her former driver, who alleged that she, Michelle Dee, and Sam Panlilio physically assaulted and illegally detained him back in January.

item.title: ‘I am deeply sorry’: Suntay’s wife breaks silence on husband's Anne Curtis remark
item.description: The wife of Rep. Bong Suntay has issued a public statement distancing herself and her children from the controversial remarks the Quezon City lawmaker made during a House impeachment hearing, after his comments referencing actress Anne Curtis sparked widespread backlash online.

item.title: No double standard in impeachment proceedings — House justice panel members
item.description: Leaders of the House Committee on Justice asserted there was no double standard in its deliberations on the impeachment complaints against the country’s two highest officials.

item.title: DSWD urges stronger cooperation among ASEAN states amid global tensions
item.description: Social Welfare Secretary Rex Gatchalian called on other members of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) for stronger cooperation amid global headwinds.

item.title: Man wanted for inciting to sedition surrenders to police
item.description: The Quezon City Police District on Thursday said it has successfully served a warrant of arrest against an individual wanted for inciting to sedition.
